title: 9 Herbal Creams to Help Alleviate Post Nasal Drip Symptoms

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for post nasal drip because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating the symptoms of post nasal drip.
Quercetin is good for post nasal drip because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and clear out mucus.

Tannins, also known as goldenseal, are good for post nasal drip because they have antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the nasal passages, thereby decreasing the production of excess mucus.

Polyphenols are good for post nasal drip because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and mucous membranes, thus alleviating congestion and excessive mucus production.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a popular remedy for post nasal drip because of its natural properties. When you breathe in, the tiny particles in the air can irritate the lining of your nose and throat, causing excess mucus to drip down the back of your throat. Eucalyptus globulus cream contains a strong-smelling oil called eucalyptol, which helps to break down and clear out these excess mucus particles. This oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. By using eucalyptus globulus cream, you can help to thin out the mucus, making it easier to cough up and reducing the feeling of post nasal drip. Additionally, the cream can also help to open up the airways, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal creams that help with post nasal drip, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Hydrastis canadensis root, also known as goldenseal, coconut oil, beeswax, and a carrier oil like sweet almond oil. First, make a herbal infusion by steeping the dried goldenseal root in hot water for about 10 minutes, then straining the liquid and letting it cool. Next, in a small saucepan, melt the coconut oil and beeswax together over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully dissolved. Once the herbal infusion has cooled, add it to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture, stirring well. Finally, add a carrier oil like sweet almond oil, which will help the cream to spread and absorb into the skin. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before using it as a cream to help alleviate post nasal drip.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for post nasal drip

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for post nasal drip because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and mucus production in the nasal passages.

Flavonoids, also known as goldenseal, are good for post nasal drip because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing mucus to drain more easily.

Polyphenols are good for post nasal drip because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort of excess mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream is often used to help with post nasal drip. This is because Echinacea is a plant that is known for its ability to boost the immune system. When you have a cold or sinus infection, your body produces excess mucus to fight the infection. This excess mucus can cause post nasal drip, where you feel like you have a constant runny nose or tickly throat. Echinacea purpurea cream is thought to help reduce this excess mucus production by stimulating the body's natural defenses. Additionally, Echinacea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses. By reducing inflammation and excess mucus production, Echinacea purpurea cream may help to alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip, such as congestion, coughing, and a runny nose.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hydrastis canadensis herbal cream that helps with post nasal drip, first gather the necessary ingredients. Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, is the key herb for this remedy. You'll also need a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. Start by making a tea by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried goldenseal root in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then let it cool. Strain the tea and mix it with 1/4 cup of carrier oil in a small bowl. Next, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating. Once the beeswax is melted, add it to the goldenseal oil mixture and stir well. Finally, add a few drops of vitamin E oil as a preservative and pour the mixture into a small tin or jar. Let it cool and harden, then apply the cream to the affected area as needed to help soothe and relieve post nasal drip symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for post nasal drip

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for post nasal drip because it has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight infections in the nasal passages.

Carvacrol in plants like Thymus species (thyme) has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce post nasal drip by killing bacteria and viruses that cause congestion and inflammation in the nasal passages.

Rosmarinic acid is good for post nasal drip because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving the symptoms of post nasal drip.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris cream is a natural remedy that can help with post-nasal drip. The main ingredient, thyme, has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the nasal passages. This can help reduce the production of excess mucus, which can lead to post-nasal drip. When applied topically as a cream, thyme can help ease congestion and clear out nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, thyme's antiseptic properties can help prevent infections, such as sinus infections, that can cause post-nasal drip. Using Thymus vulgaris cream can provide quick relief from the discomfort and annoyance of post-nasal drip.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hydrastis canadensis herbal cream that helps with post nasal drip, first you need to gather the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Hydrastis canadensis root, coconut oil, beeswax, and a few other natural ingredients. Next, infuse the dried root in a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, by placing it in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the liquid and discard the solids. Then, melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the beeswax has melted, add the infused oil to the mixture and stir well. You can also add other natural ingredients, such as vitamin E oil or lavender oil, to enhance the cream's properties. Continue to heat the mixture until it reaches the desired consistency, then remove it from the heat and let it cool and thicken. Once the cream has cooled, transfer it to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to prevent spoilage. Apply the cream to your nostrils and the roof of your mouth as needed to help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for post nasal drip

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for post nasal drip because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier drainage and relief from excess mucus.
Flavonoids are good for post nasal drip because they help reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion.

Polyphenols are good for post nasal drip because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for better drainage and relief from excess mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a natural remedy for post nasal drip. This condition happens when excess mucus drips from the nose, causing discomfort. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has been used for centuries to soothe and calm the respiratory system. The cream is made from this root and is easily absorbed into the skin. When applied to the nose and face, it helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause mucus buildup. Glycyrrhiza glabra has anti-inflammatory properties that ease congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, its natural moisturizing properties keep the skin around the nose and mouth hydrated, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by post nasal drip. By using Glycyrrhiza glabra cream, you can help your body fight off infections and reduce symptoms of post nasal drip naturally.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Hydrastis canadensis herbal cream that helps with post nasal drip, start by infusing 2 tablespoons of dried Goldenseal root in 1 cup of warm water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid and let it cool. Then, mix the cooled liquid with 1 tablespoon of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture until the beeswax melts, and then add 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil and 1 teaspoon of aloe vera gel. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and cools. Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and let it set at room temperature for a few hours. Once set, your Hydrastis canadensis herbal cream is ready to use. To apply, gently rub a small amount onto your nasal passages and surrounding skin to help reduce post nasal drip and soothe any discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for post nasal drip

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: I couldn't find any information on the bioactive compound Rosmarinic acid in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x7d11d7b375b0> Hydrastis canadensis being related to the plant I think you meant is Hydrastis canadensis is known as goldenseal, and it is not typically associated with Rosmarinic acid but I did find information that the actual bioactive compound in goldenseal is Berberine and Berberine has anti-inflammatory properties which may help to reduce postnasal drip by reducing swelling in the nasal passages.

Polyphenols are good for post nasal drip because they have antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating symptoms of post nasal drip.
Tannins help to reduce post nasal drip by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and astringent, thereby drying up excess mucus and reducing congestion in the nasal passages.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream is a natural remedy that may help with post nasal drip. This cream is made from the leaves of the rosemary plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you have post nasal drip, the inside of your nose can become irritated and inflamed, causing excess mucus to flow down the back of your throat. The anti-inflammatory properties in the rosemary plant help to reduce this inflammation, making it easier for your body to fight off the underlying infection or allergy causing the post nasal drip. The active compounds in rosemary, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, have decongestant properties that can help to break down mucus and reduce its production. This can provide relief from the discomfort and congestion associated with post nasal drip. Additionally, rosemary has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to post nasal drip. Using a Rosmarinus officinalis cream on the nasal area can help to open up the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing the amount of mucus that flows down the back of the throat. It can also help to soothe and calm the inflamed tissues in the nose, providing temporary relief from the discomfort and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal cream using Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, to help with post nasal drip, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried goldenseal root, coconut oil, beeswax, and a few drops of essential oil like peppermint or eucalyptus for added fragrance and benefits. Begin by infusing the goldenseal root in a carrier oil like coconut oil in a double boiler, allowing the mixture to simmer for several hours to draw out the active compounds. Once the infusion has cooled, strain it and discard the solids. Then, melt the beeswax in a separate double boiler, and slowly mix it with the cooled goldenseal infusion. Stir well to combine, and pour the mixture into small containers. Allow the mixture to cool and solidify, then add a few drops of essential oil on top. Your homemade goldenseal cream is now ready to use, simply apply a small amount to the affected area to help soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing post nasal drip.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy for post nasal drip. The menthol in peppermint creams helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel from your nose and sinuses. When you apply peppermint creams to your nose and chest, the menthol gets absorbed into the skin and then travels to the nasal passages. This helps to calm the blood vessels and reduce swelling in the nasal lining, making it less likely for mucus to build up. Additionally, the menthol in peppermint has antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections in the nasal passages. As you breathe in the menthol from the cream, it can also help to clear your airways and reduce congestion. This can help to provide quick relief from post nasal drip, allowing you to breathe more easily and feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hydrastis canadensis herbal cream that can help with post nasal drip, start by gathering the required ingredients: dried goldenseal root (Hydrastis canadensis), a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, a beeswax base, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. First, prepare the goldenseal root by mixing 1 teaspoon of dried goldenseal root with 2 cups of boiling water. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid and set it aside to cool. Next, mix the cooled goldenseal liquid with 1/2 cup of carrier oil in a bowl. In a separate pot, melt 1/4 cup of beeswax base over low heat. Once the beeswax is fully melted, slowly add it to the goldenseal-oil mixture, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy consistency. Finally, add a few drops of vitamin E oil as a preservative and pour the cream into a clean container. Store the cream in the refrigerator to slow down bacterial growth and use it as needed to help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for post nasal drip

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger cream, made from Zingiber officinale, is a natural remedy that can help with post nasal drip. Ginger cream helps to soothe and calm this inflammation, reducing the amount of mucus produced. Additionally, ginger has natural decongestant properties that help to clear out excess mucus and debris from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and swallow. When applied topically, the cream helps to warm up the nasal area, making it easier for mucus to drain out, rather than building up and causing more discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal creams that help with post nasal drip, first, you need to make an herbal infusion of goldenseal root. This involves mixing one ounce of dried goldenseal root with four cups of boiling water, and letting it steep for 10-15 minutes. After straining the mixture, combine the liquid with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:3 ratio, and let it sit for a few hours to allow the liquid to infuse into the oil. Once the oil has cooled, mix it with a base cream or lotion, such as aloe vera or shea butter, in a 1:2 ratio. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil to help preserve the cream and prevent spoilage. Finally, transfer the mixture to a clean container and store it in the fridge for up to a week. Apply a small amount of the cream to the affected area as needed to help soothe and reduce post nasal drip symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams are a good remedy for post nasal drip because they help to soothe and calm the nasal passages. The active ingredients in Salvia officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal cavity. This helps to prevent excess mucus from dripping down the back of the throat, providing relief from the discomfort and irritation associated with post nasal drip. Additionally, Salvia officinalis creams have decongestant properties that help to clear out mucus and other debris from the nasal passages, further reducing the symptoms of post nasal drip. By using Salvia officinalis creams, you can help to thin out excess mucus and promote a healthy balance of moisture in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving the symptoms of post nasal drip.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal creams for post nasal drip relief, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: Hydrastis canadensis root, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin E oil. Begin by preparing the Hydrastis canadensis root by cleaning and drying it, then grinding it into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine 2-3 tablespoons of the powdered root with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Heat the mixture gently, stirring occasionally, until the coconut oil has melted and the mixture has reached a temperature of around 160-180°F. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches around 120-130°F, then stir in 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture has thickened into a smooth, creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify completely before using the herbal cream as needed to help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, is a natural remedy that has been used to help with post nasal drip. The cream made from this plant contains a compound called berberine, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. When you have post nasal drip, the mucous membranes in your nose and throat become inflamed, leading to excess mucus production. The berberine in goldenseal cream helps to reduce this inflammation and fight off any underlying infections that may be causing the symptoms. By applying the cream topically to the affected area, you can help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing the amount of mucus being produced and providing relief from congestion and discomfort. Additionally, the cream may also help to thin out the mucus, making it easier to expel and clear out the nasal passages. This can help to provide quick and effective relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal creams for post nasal drip, start by gathering dried goldenseal root. This herb contains berberine, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the nasal passages. First, you'll need to make a goldenseal tincture by steeping one ounce of dried goldenseal root in one pint of vodka for two weeks, shaking the mixture daily. After the steeping time is up, strain the mixture and discard the solids. Mix the tincture with other ingredients like a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and essential oils like peppermint or eucalyptus to create a soothing and protective cream. Heat the oil and tincture mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until the beeswax is fully melted. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency that's thick but still spreadable. Pour the cream into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool completely before using. Apply a small amount of the cream to the nasal area as needed to help reduce post nasal drip and soothe the nasal passages.

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful remedy for post-nasal drip, a common condition where mucus drips down the back of the throat. Eucalyptus globulus, an essential oil extracted from the eucalyptus tree, has natural decongestant properties that can help reduce congestion and relieve the discomfort caused by post-nasal drip. It works by opening up airways and loosening mucus, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Echinacea purpurea, a plant-based herb, is known for its immune-boosting properties and can help fight off underlying infections that may be causing the post-nasal drip. By using a topical herbal cream containing these ingredients, you can target the affected area directly and experience relief from the symptoms. Fixing post-nasal drip is crucial because it can lead to sore throats, coughing, and difficulty sleeping. If left untreated, it can also lead to respiratory problems and sinus infections. By addressing the issue, you can improve your quality of life by reducing discomfort, increasing energy levels, and enhancing overall well-being. You can breathe easier, sleep better, and enjoy daily activities without the constant feeling of congestion.
supplementary_best: To remedy post-nasal drip, a combination of Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Glycyrrhiza glabra is beneficial. Eucalyptus globulus is a natural decongestant that helps reduce nasal congestion. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, is an antimicrobial herb that fights off infections that can cause post-nasal drip. Its expectorant properties also help loosen and clear mucus. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, is soothing and anti-inflammatory, helping to calm the nasal passages and reduce irritation. This combination of decongestant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties makes it effective in addressing the root causes of post-nasal drip.
supplementary_avoid: If you have post nasal drip, you should avoid using herbal creams that contain peppermint (Mentha piperita) or wintergreen (Gaultheria procumbens) because they can make the condition worse. These herbs have a cooling effect, which can stimulate the nasal passages and make you produce more mucus. This can lead to a cycle of increased congestion and post nasal drip. Additionally, some herbal creams may contain chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla), which, while soothing in small amounts, can cause an allergic reaction in some people, exacerbating the condition. Similarly, creams with sage (Salvia officinalis) should be avoided, as it can cause stomach upset if ingested, and may not be suitable for people who have stomach issues.
supplementary_other_remedies: For people who experience post nasal drip, there are several natural remedies that can provide relief. One such remedy is neti pots. These small pots are used to rinse the nasal passages with a saline solution, which helps to clear out mucus and reduce congestion. This is because the saline solution helps to loosen and flush out the thick mucus that's causing the drip. Using a neti pot regularly can help to keep nasal passages clean and reduce the occurrence of post nasal drip.  Another natural remedy is steam inhalation. When you breathe in steam, it helps to loosen and thin out mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel. This can be done by taking a hot shower, inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water, or using a humidifier in your home. The moisture from the steam helps to break down the mucus, making it easier to get rid of. This can provide quick relief from post nasal drip.  Drinking plenty of fluids, especially warm liquids like tea or broth, can also help to thin out mucus and reduce post nasal drip. When you're dehydrated, your body produces thicker mucus, which can exacerbate the problem. By drinking plenty of fluids, you can help to keep your mucus thin and make it easier to get rid of. This can be especially helpful when you're experiencing a cold or sinus infection.  Elevating your head while sleeping can also help to reduce post nasal drip. When you sleep with your head at a higher elevation, it can help to prevent mucus from flowing down the back of your throat, which can reduce congestion and discomfort. This is because gravity helps to keep mucus in the nasal passages, rather than letting it flow down into the throat.  Additionally, saline nasal sprays can be used to help moisturize and protect the nasal passages. These sprays contain a solution of salt and water, which can help to thin out mucus and reduce inflammation. They can be used as needed to provide quick relief from post nasal drip.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with post nasal drip often experience other health issues. One common problem is sinusitis, an inflammation of the sinuses. This is because post nasal drip can lead to bacterial growth, which causes infection and swelling in the sinuses. Medicinal herbs like eucalyptus and peppermint can help reduce sinus pressure and congestion. They work by opening up the airways and loosening mucus, making it easier to breathe.  Another issue people with post nasal drip may face is allergies. Allergies can trigger post nasal drip, and in turn, the condition can worsen allergy symptoms. Herbs like nettle and quercetin have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate allergy symptoms. They reduce histamine release, which is responsible for allergic reactions.  Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) is another common condition associated with post nasal drip. When mucus drips down the back of the throat, it can cause stomach acid to flow up into the esophagus, leading to heartburn and acid reflux. Herbs like licorice root and slippery elm can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, providing relief from GERD symptoms.  Throat infections, such as tonsillitis, can also be a problem for people with post nasal drip. The constant dripping of mucus can irritate the throat and make it more susceptible to infection. Herbs like echinacea and sage can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to fight off infections.  Finally, people with post nasal drip may experience fatigue and exhaustion. The constant discomfort and congestion can make it difficult to get a good night's sleep, leading to fatigue. Herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to get the restful sleep needed to combat fatigue.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for post nasal drip
